data.narrative:
Cresilon is a Series B biotech & health company founded by Joe Landolina, having raised $113M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 8 private peers (including Chai Discovery at $3.8B, Colossal Biosciences at $10.3B, Eikon Therapeutics at $539M, and New Limit at $3.1B) and 3 public sector benchmarks (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L).

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement, yielding $215M based on the $113M raised proxy. Peer-median valuation across the 4 same-sector and same/adjacent-stage comparables generates $3.5B, reflecting a range of $539M to $10.3B. Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied: while the Biotech & Health sector shows a 6.9x price-to-sales median, Cresilon's revenue is not on file, precluding meaningful calculation.

The two methods produce divergent signals. The stage-progression method anchors to capital deployed; the peer-median incorporates market pricing of similar-stage competitors. The triangulated analysis supports a range of $182M (low) to $4.0B (high), with a median of $1.8B. Key uncertainties include whether Cresilon's pipeline and clinical progress track toward the upper or lower end of peer valuations, and whether recent financing terms provide anchoring data for post-money valuation.
